title: OOF hierarchical target encoding for driver/race/compound context
summary: Current best runs use frequency encodings and category crosses, but not target-aware encodings.
rationale: Current best runs use frequency encodings and category crosses, but not target-aware encodings. CatBoost’s ordered/CTR-style handling is specifically designed for high-cardinality categorical combinations and should capture stable pit-tendency priors such as Driver x Compound x Stint or Driver x Race x Year more directly than raw frequencies.
implementation_hint: Build only out-of-fold encodings, ideally chronological within each Year-Race-Driver sequence or at minimum GroupKFold by race. Start with smoothed means for Driver, Driver+Compound, Driver+Race, Race+Compound, and Driver+Compound+Stint; compare adding them to the existing booster versus a CatBoost model that natively learns CTRs.
expected_effect: Moderate CV lift if hidden pit-propensity priors exist beyond the current freq features; likely strongest on rare drivers/races where simple frequency counts are too weak.
risk: High leakage risk if encodings are fit on full data or across same-race fold boundaries; gains may vanish if the current categorical interactions already cover most of this signal.
